rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope or properly declared as constants/expressions.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning declared types with expression types in binary comparisons and assignments.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign only for output features or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex or component scope.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constant and equation declarations.
Validate that binary expressions in guarantees use compatible types (int, real, or bool) on both sides.
Prefer visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid <error> type diagnostics.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE must declare valid types (int, real, bool) and be type-compatible in expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current AGREE annex scope.
Do not mix incompatible types (e.g., int and real) in binary comparison expressions without explicit conversion.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope unless they are tied to output features or auxiliary variables.
